questions.text: Which of the following metals is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that remains in a liquid state at room temperature.

questions.text: The property of metals by which they can be drawn into thin wires is called:
questions.explanation: Ductility is the ability of a material to be stretched into wires. Gold is the most ductile metal.

questions.text: Which non-metal is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Graphite, an allotrope of carbon, is an exception among non-metals as it conducts electricity due to free electrons.

questions.text: Metal oxides which react with both acids as well as bases to produce salt and water are called:
questions.explanation: Amphoteric oxides like Aluminium oxide (Al2O3) show both acidic and basic behavior.

questions.text: Aqua regia is a freshly prepared mixture of concentrated hydrochloric acid and concentrated nitric acid in the ratio:
questions.explanation: Aqua regia consists of Conc. HCl and Conc. HNO3 in a 3:1 ratio. It is capable of dissolving gold and platinum.
